reasoning: Sevilla's historical base rate of winning La Liga is incredibly low (only 1 win historically, almost 80 years ago, compared to the dominance of Real Madrid, Barcelona, and Atletico Madrid). Pre-season odds and analytical models (like Opta) placed them as having zero chance to win, with up to a 23.7% chance to be relegated instead. Polymarket itself priced this outcome at 0.00 a week prior to the forecast date. Their current standing and squad quality are nowhere near title-contending level, making a title win practically impossible. As per the constraints, I assign the lowest possible bounding probability: 2%.
